instruction: Help me summarize the HR headcount

command: DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset s off 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset -dpms 2>/dev/null; true

python3 -c "
import openpyxl
wb = openpyxl.Workbook()
ws = wb.active
ws['A1'] = 'Department'
ws['B1'] = 'Headcount'
ws['C1'] = 'New Hires(Year)'
ws['D1'] = 'Departures(Year)'
ws['A2'] = 'R&D Dept'
ws['B2'] = 45
ws['C2'] = 8
ws['D2'] = 3
ws['A3'] = 'Sales Dept'
ws['B3'] = 32
ws['C3'] = 12
ws['D3'] = 7
ws['A4'] = 'Operations Dept'
ws['B4'] = 28
ws['C4'] = 5
ws['D4'] = 2
ws['A5'] = 'Finance Dept'
ws['B5'] = 15
ws['C5'] = 2
ws['D5'] = 1
wb.save('/home/user/Desktop/hr_headcount.xlsx')
"

phases.instruction: Help me summarize the HR headcount

Find the department with the highest net headcount increase (hires minus departures) from /home/user/Desktop/hr_headcount.xlsx, append to /home/user/Desktop/headcount_note.txt: 'Highest Net Increase Dept: Sales Dept (net +5)', save

include: Highest Net Increase Dept: Sales Dept (net +5)
